claim: German sectoral co-determination (Mitbestimmung) and workers-on-boards has coexisted with competitive export-manufacturing performance 1976-present, refuting the claim that worker voice necessarily reduces firm competitiveness.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: German sectoral co-determination (Mitbestimmung) and workers-on-boards has coexisted with competitive export-manufacturing performance 1976-present, refuting the claim that worker voice necessarily reduces firm competitiveness.

claim: Biden's IRA/CHIPS industrial policy will show partial success on capacity-building metrics and mixed results on job creation, consistent with the conditional view that industrial policy works where targeting is technically competent and governance is strong.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Biden's 2022 IRA and CHIPS Act — strategic subsidies for semiconductor and clean-energy manufacturing — will produce measurable domestic capacity and productivity gains by 2030 in targeted sectors.

Lula third-term's Nova Indústria Brasil 2024 industrial-policy package, conditioned on export performance and technology-diffusion metrics, produces measurable sectoral capability gains (semiconductors, green hydrogen, health-industrial complex) by 2030 — replicating the East Asian export-discipline conditionality pattern rather than the earlier Latin American ISI pattern.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Developmentalist prediction: conditioned industrial policy works; unconditioned industrial policy (Dilma-era nova matriz, earlier Brazilian ISI) does not. Coding requires identifying which pattern Nova Indústria Brasil implements. Audit FAIL vs industrial_policy_semiconductor_chips_act_effectiveness (entity/geography mismatch: claim is Lula Brazil 2024 while hypothesis tests Biden IRA/CHIPS). Phase 4C candidate for new hypothesis: nova_industria_brasil_export_discipline_pattern_effect.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Biden's IRA/CHIPS industrial policy will show partial success on capacity-building metrics and mixed results on job creation, consistent with the conditional view that industrial policy works where targeting is technically competent and governance is strong.

claim: Tariff protection for infant industries under export-discipline conditionality (Korea steel, Brazil Embraer) produces capability build-up that free-trade-from-start counterfactuals would not have generated in the same timeframe.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Tariff protection for infant industries under export-discipline conditionality (Korea steel, Brazil Embraer) produces capability build-up that free-trade-from-start counterfactuals would not have generated in the same timeframe.

claim: Major trade-liberalisation events — defined as sharp, policy-driven increases in trade-to-GDP openness sustained over at least 5 years — are associated with positive subsequent per-capita GDP growth over 10-year horizons, relative to matched non-liberalising controls. The headline canonical cases examined are China's WTO accession (2001), India's 1991 liberalisation (Manmohan Singh reforms), New Zealand's 1984-1991 Rogernomics reforms, Vietnam's Doi Moi (1986 formally; main tariff reductions 1995-2007), and Chile's 1974-1979 liberalisation. The associational claim is that the sign and magnitude of subsequent growth differences are consistent with the Sachs-Warner and Wacziarg-Welch literatures and are not driven by selection into liberalisation alone.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Trade liberalisation episodes (China post-1978, India 1991, Mexico NAFTA 1994) are followed by per-capita output growth acceleration, not deceleration, in the liberalising economy.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope names NAFTA (Mexico) which is absent from hypothesis canonical cases (hypothesis names China WTO, India 1991, NZ Rogernomics, Vietnam Doi Moi, Chile 1974). Phase 4C: candidate for relink to a NAFTA-inclusive hypothesis or rewrite to match existing canonical cases.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Trade liberalisation episodes (China 1978, India 1991, NZ Rogernomics, EU single market 1993, China WTO 2001) are followed within a decade by per-capita income acceleration relative to counterfactual non-reform paths.
covers_claims_inbound.claim_notes: Audit flagged as FAIL — claim scope names eu_single_market_1993 and deng_reforms_1978 as canonical cases; linked hypothesis canonical cases are China WTO 2001, India 1991, NZ Rogernomics, Vietnam Doi Moi, Chile 1974–1979 (EU single market and Deng 1978 absent). Phase 4C: candidate for relink (EU single-market claim could split to eu_single_market_productivity_and_trade_gains hypothesis) or hypothesis augmentation.

claim: Trump tariff policy 2018-2019 did not reshore US manufacturing output to a meaningful degree, and tariff incidence fell on US importers and consumers, consistent with post-Keynesian emphasis on demand-side rather than price-competition factors in trade flows.

covers_claims_inbound.claim_text: Trump tariff policy 2018-2019 did not reshore US manufacturing output to a meaningful degree, and tariff incidence fell on US importers and consumers, consistent with post-Keynesian emphasis on demand-side rather than price-competition factors in trade flows.
